summary: Excellent Owsley soundboard (matrix‑like) recording of the Oregon Jam I, with a rough first set that improves after “Candyman”.
review: The tape captures an outstanding Owsley SBD source – listeners rave about its crystal‑clear clarity and even mistake it for a matrix. Musically the show starts shakily with feedback problems in the first set, but picks up momentum around “Candyman” and delivers a solid mid‑to‑late‑set performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is praised as excellent, while the show receives mixed feedback (strong later moments but a rough start), resulting in an overall rating near four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great clarity and fidelity; many note it sounds better than other sources and even resembles a matrix. No major drop‑outs reported, though the first set suffers from feedback onstage.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is hampered by feedback and a rough start; energy improves around “Candyman” and the later portion of the concert feels more engaging.
